data.title: Alkhorayef Water & Power Technologies Bags SAR62 Million Leak Detection, Repair Contract
data.description:
Alkhorayef Water and Power Technologies (SASE:2081) secured a contract worth 61.8 million Saudi riyals from National Water Co., according to a Thursday release.

The water and wastewater management company will provide leak detection and repair services in Jazan, Saudi Arabia, for 30 months.

HK-listed Geely Automobile jumps on robust 2024 profit growth
data.description:
** Shares of Geely Automobile Holdings jump 4.8% to HK$18.84, their highest since February 28, and on course for third consecutive session of gains

** Stock set for the biggest one-day rise since February 27; second-biggest gainer on Hang Seng China Enterprises Index , which falls 2%

** Geely said its 2024 profit attributable jumped 213% y/y with revenue up 34% y/y and co proposed to pay a final dividend of HK$0.33 per share vs HK$0.22 each a year ago

** Hang Seng Automobile Index slips 0.3%, Hang Seng Index falls 1.8%

** Stock up 23.8% YTD

Power Assets' 2024 Attributable Profit Inches Up 2% as Revenue Falls; Shares Slide 3%
data.description:
Power Assets attributable profit inched up 2% to HK$6.12 billion in 2024 from HK$6 billion in 2023, according to a Wednesday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

Earnings per share at the energy-related investment holding company increased to HK$2.87 from HK$2.82 a year earlier. Analysts at Visible Alpha estimated EPS per share at HK$2.95.

Revenue declined to HK$919 million from HK$1.29 billion a year earlier. Visible Alpha analysts forecast revenue at HK$1.23 billion.

The company declared a final dividend of HK$2.04 per share, payable June 10 to shareholders on record as of May 27.

Shares fell 3% during Thursday's afternoon trading.

data.title: Fed's Reaction Function Seen as Complicated — Market Talk
data.description: The Federal Reserve's reaction function is really complicated, says Global X's Scott Helfstein. The Fed extended its pause on interest rates, as expected, raised its inflation forecasts and lowered growth projections. "They have to grapple with rising risk to both mandates, employment and inflation simultaneously," says the head of investment strategy. This isn't stagflation in the traditional sense, he says, adding that policy is having an outsize impact on consumer and business expectations. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Gulf General Cooperative Insurance to Submit Plan to Boost Solvency Margin
data.description:
Gulf General Cooperative Insurance (SASE:8260) said Thursday that it will file a plan to solve and boost its declining solvency margin to Saudi's Insurance Authority.

The insurer's move follows a notice from the regulator regarding its solvency margin being below its required threshold. Gulf General said it will have three working days to submit the plan.

data.title: Sany Heavy Chairman Proposes Up to 2-Billion-Yuan Buyback Program
data.description:
Sany Heavy Industry received a proposal from its chair, Xiang Wenbo, for the repurchase of between 1 billion yuan and 2 billion yuan of shares.

Under the proposal, the shares will be bought back via centralized bidding within 12 months from board approval, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of the heavy machinery company were down 1% in recent trade.

RWE Raises Dividend After Earnings Declined But Beat Expectations
data.description:
(06:00 GMT) *RWE 2024 Adj Net Pft EUR2.32B
(06:00 GMT) *RWE 2024 Adj Group Ebitda EUR5.68B
(06:00 GMT) *RWE 2024 Adj EBIT EUR3.56B
(06:00 GMT) *Analysts Had Forecast RWE Adj Net Pft at EUR2.22B
(06:00 GMT) *Analysts Had Forecast RWE Adj Ebitda at EUR5.5B
(06:00 GMT) *RWE Raises 2024 Dividend to EUR1.10
(06:00 GMT) *RWE Targets 2025 Adj Ebitda in EUR4.55B to EUR5.15B Range
(06:00 GMT) *RWE Targets 2025 Adj EBIT in EUR2.35B to EUR2.95B Range
(06:00 GMT) *RWE Targets 2025 Adj Net Pft in EUR1.3B to EUR1.8B Range
(06:00 GMT) *RWE Intends to Raise Dividend in 2025 to EUR1.20 Per Share
(06:00 GMT) *RWE Share Buyback Expected Completed by 2Q 2026
(06:35 GMT) RWE Raises Dividend After Earnings Declined But Beat Expectations

By Pierre Bertrand

RWE raised its dividend after its 2024 earnings fell but beat analysts' expectations.

The German energy company said it achieved 2.32 billion euros ($2.53 billion) in adjusted net profit for last year, compared with 4.1 billion euros a year prior.

Adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization came to 5.68 billion euros, from 7.75 billion euros in 2023.

It compares with analysts' expectations of 2.22 billion euros in adjusted net profit from 5.5 billion euros in adjusted Ebitda, according to a company-provided consensus.

External revenue, meanwhile, declined on year to 24.22 billion euros, reflecting lower contributions from its offshore wind and flexible-generation divisions.

RWE said it would propose a 1.10 euros per share dividend for 2024, up 0.10 euros from 2023, adding that it intends to raise its dividend further to 1.20 euros for 2025.

RWE added that it expects that margins from electricity sales, will normalize this year. It also expects normalized earnings at its supply and trading segment.

The company said that for this year it is targeting between 1.3 billion euros and 1.8 billion euros in adjusted net profit, and is targeting between 4.55 billion and 5.15 billion euros in adjusted Ebitda.

Write to Pierre Bertrand at pierre.bertrand@wsj.com

data.title: Kemira Invests In Thailand, Raises Production Capacity For Board, Paper Chemicals
data.description:
Kemira Oyj :

  • INVESTS IN THAILAND, INCREASES PRODUCTION CAPACITY FOR BOARD AND PAPER CHEMICALS

  • INVESTS SEVERAL MILLION EUROS IN EXPANSION OF PRODUCTION LINES AT WELLGROWN PRODUCTION FACILITY

data.title: Kemira Plans Multi-million-euro Boost for Production of Paper, Board Chemical in Thailand
data.description:
Finnish chemical company Kemira unveiled Thursday a multi-million-euro plan to expand its production in Thailand of chemical agents for paper, board and tissue.

The capital will be used to boost capacity at its Wellgrow site, with implementation expected to start in 2026. The new capacity is anticipated to start operations in August of the same year, boosting the site's total estimated annual capacity to 100,000 tons.

data.title: Norske Skog's French Unit to Start Production of Recycled Containerboard in April
data.description:
Norske Skog said Thursday that its Norske Skog Golbey business unit will begin production of recycled containerboard at its site in France in April.

The Norwegian pulp and paper company said the unit is now finalizing the pre-commissioning of the machine that will be used for the production.

data.description:

** Stock on track for best day since April 2024, if trends hold

** GPTI eyes busiest trading session in over six weeks, volumes at 3.3x the 30-day avg

** Year-to-date, stock down ~33%

($1 = 86.2900 Indian rupees)
